title: io.denna.legal.terms-of-service
description: Canonical Terms of Service document for a website that interacts with on-chain protocols. Captures operator identity, governing law, jurisdictional restrictions, structured section content, and definitions. Designed to be rendered by a frontend, surfaced in an acceptance modal, fetched by external auditors, and machine-verified via signature. Enforcement of geographic blocking lives in `io.denna.defi.vault-config` (compliance.geoBlocking) and is referenced from this document for display only.

$schema.description: URI or relative path to this schema.

title.description: Document title as displayed to users (e.g., "Terms of Service").

effectiveDate.description: Date this version of the Terms became effective.

supersedes.description: ID (metadata.id) of the prior Terms document this one supersedes.

operator.description: Identity of the entity operating the Site (the "Site Operator").

name.description: Legal or trading name of the operator (e.g., "Keel Foundation").

domain.description: Primary domain at which the Site is hosted (e.g., "keel.fi").

contact.description: Email address for legal notices and contact.

governingLaw.description: Jurisdiction whose law governs this document (e.g., "Cayman Islands").

jurisdiction.description: Optional dispute-resolution metadata. Display copy lives in the corresponding section blocks; this field is for tooling and indexing.

restrictedJurisdictions.description: Display list of jurisdictions excluded under the Geographic Restrictions section. Note: enforcement lives in `io.denna.defi.vault-config` under compliance.geoBlocking; this list is informational and may include non-ISO regions (e.g., comprehensively sanctioned sub-regions).

code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, or an uppercase identifier for sub-regions (e.g., "CRIMEA").

name.description: Human-readable name (e.g., "United States of America", "Crimea Region").

summary.description: Short blocks rendered in an acceptance modal or preview surface before the user navigates to the full document.

sections.description: Ordered top-level sections of the document.

definitions.description: Defined terms used throughout the document. Renderers may surface these as a glossary.

section.description: A top-level section. Sections do not nest in this schema; use ordered/lettered IDs in the title for hierarchy (e.g., "19. Dispute Resolution").

id.description: Stable kebab-case anchor used in URLs (e.g., "governing-law").

title.description: Section heading as displayed to users.

block.description: A renderable block. Either a paragraph (a sequence of styled spans) or a list (bullet or ordered, with each item containing spans).

style.description: Marker style. `bullet` renders as an unordered list; `ordered` renders as a numbered list.

span.description: An inline text run. Renderers MUST treat `text` as plain text (no HTML, no Markdown). Optional `style` adds emphasis. Optional `href` turns the span into a link; the href is rendered verbatim by the consumer, who is responsible for any same-origin or scheme allowlisting.

text.description: Plain text content of the span.

style.description: Inline emphasis. `code` is intended for inline monospace (e.g., a domain or address).

href.description: If set, the span renders as a link. Accepts absolute URLs, relative paths, or `mailto:` URIs. Consumers SHOULD validate the scheme against an allowlist before rendering.
